name: Congee with Bok Choy (Two Rice)
recipeIngredient: 1 large bok choy, head separated into white stalks and leaves
1 teaspoon scallion oil (peanut oil infused with green onions)
recipeInstructions:
1. Place both kinds of rice in a large pot. Wash the rice under water three times, rubbing between hands and draining each time.
2. Return the rice to the pot, add cold water, cover, and bring to boil. Leave the lid open slightly. Reduce heat to medium-low and cook for 1 to 1 1/2 hours, stirring occasionally to prevent rice from sticking to the bottom of the pot. Cook until the rice thickens almost to the consistency of porridge.
3. As the rice is cooking, wash bok choy stalks and leaves. Cut the leaves into 1/4-by-4-inch slices and stalks into 1/4-inch pieces.
4. About 7 minutes before the rice is completely cooked, add the bok choy stalks, salt, soy sauce, pepper, and ginger to the congee. Mix together thoroughly and bring the congee to a boil, stirring constantly.
5. Reduce heat, add the bok choy leaves, and cook for 2 to 3 minutes longer.
6. Turn off the heat, then add scallion oil and stir well into the mixture.
7. Serve hot. This recipe serves 4 to 6 people.
Original source: From the Earth: Chinese Cooking by Eileen Yin-Fei Lo (MacMillan). Reviewed by Press-Enterprise, 1996 Ap 10 Riverside. MC by phannema@wizard.ucr.edu.
